target.name: Tekever
target.founder: Ricardo Mendes, Vitor Cristina, Pedro Sinogas

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $631M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Percepto
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Tekever operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector at Series C stage, having raised $631M+ to date. The comparable universe consists of six private firms in the same sector, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comps exist in this narrow category.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum sample thresholds—only one adjacent-stage peer was identified despite five total sector peers existing. Consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-subsequent-stage financing rounds. Applied to the $631M capital raised proxy, this yields an implied valuation of $1.1B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $912M to $1.2B, with a median point of $1.1B. This range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression output but rests on a single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the 1.7x multiplier to Tekever's specific capital efficiency and market conditions, and the absence of direct peer valuation data to cross-validate the implied range. A fuller triangulation would require either access to recent private valuations of adjacent-stage drones companies or emergence of public comparables in autonomous systems.
